title: Huge endangered snapping turtle, somehow loose in Mass., rescued from pond
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: An endangered alligator snapping turtle, illegal to keep in Massachusetts, was found in an Essex County pond.
The 60-pound turtle, native to warmer climates, was lethargic with pneumonia due to cold water temperatures.
The turtle is receiving treatment at New England Wildlife Center and may be relocated to its native range.

title: After state trooper killed, Senate approves amendment aimed at deterring wrong-way drivers
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Massachusetts Senate unanimously approved measures to deter wrong-way drivers after Trooper Kevin Trainor's death.
The amendment mandates MassDOT to implement infrastructure and warning systems to prevent wrong-way driving incidents.
New protocols include directional striping, signage, and training for police on handling wrong-way driving cases.

title: Thoughts on owning one gas and one electric vehicle
author: John Paul
bullets: A hybrid garage with one gas and one electric vehicle is practical for various travel needs.
Home charging options are critical for electric vehicle practicality, such as the MSI Smart Charger.
John Paul, AAA Northeast's Car Doctor, discussed vehicle maintenance topics beyond electric vehicle inquiries.

title: Red Sox top prospect Franklin Arias makes major leap in latest ESPN rankings
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox prospect Franklin Arias rose from No. 36 to No. 6 in ESPN's top 50 rankings.
Arias, 20, is excelling with Double-A Portland, hitting .328 with 8 home runs and 25 RBIs in 33 games.
Signed from Venezuela in 2023, Arias could advance to Triple-A Worcester if performance stays strong.

title: Police respond to threat made against Perkins School for the Blind
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Watertown police responded to a threat against Perkins School for the Blind, leading to a temporary lockdown.
Police and school staff assessed the online threat, resolving the situation with no current danger reported.
The matter is being handled internally by police, with no criminal charges filed and no further details released.

title: Second arrest made in alleged sexual abuse of dog in Connecticut
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Damon Mesite, 28, was arrested in Meriden, Connecticut for charges related to aiding sexual assault of an animal.
Kristiina Kennedy, 45, was previously charged with sexually assaulting her dog and creating pornographic material of the acts.
Kennedy's electronic devices allegedly contained images of prohibited sexual contact with her two cats.

title: Bruins reportedly moving on from one of their assistant coaches
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins are not renewing assistant coach Jay Leach's contract this offseason.
Leach managed Boston's defense and penalty kill, which struggled in the 2024-25 and 2025-26 seasons.
Marco Sturm, the head coach, will now choose a new assistant to oversee the Bruins' defense.

title: NFL insider would be ‘surprised’ if Patriots don’t land A.J. Brown
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: NFL insider Ian Rapoport believes the Patriots are likely to acquire A.J. Brown but no deal is confirmed yet.
The Eagles want a 2027 first-round pick for A.J. Brown; negotiations might involve other Patriots' players and picks.
Patriots' crowded receiver room includes new signing Romeo Doubs and potential trade inclusion, Kayshon Boutte.

title: Here’s how much a last-minute trip to Cape Cod for Memorial Day weekend costs
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: A day trip to Cape Cod costs approximately $162.75, including $72 for gas and $39 for a lobster roll.
A couple's getaway from Friday to Monday costs around $1,867.55, with accommodations at The Villages at Ocean Edge.
A family vacation at Bayside Resort Hotel in West Yarmouth is estimated at $2,331.35, including $1,083.27 for lodging.

title: 5 arrested in brawl at Dorchester courthouse
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Five individuals, including a 17-year-old, arrested following a brawl at Dorchester Municipal Court on Wednesday morning.
The suspects face charges of affray and disrupting court proceedings; additional charges filed against the 17-year-old.
Three adults plead not guilty and are held on $1,000 bail; Johnson's arraignment was rescheduled.
